articles.title: Musk vs. Altman, and a Pivotal Immigration Case at the Supreme Court
articles.summary: Plus, why nursing homes are hiring M.M.A. fighters.

articles.title: In France, American Universities Lose Their Allure in the Trump Era
articles.summary: Worries about visas, academic freedom and safety are making foreign schools, like Sciences Po in Paris, more attractive to some students than the Ivy League.

articles.title: At State Dinner, King Charles Charms the Court of Trump
articles.summary: King Charles III presented President Trump with a golden bell. “Should you ever need to get hold of us,” the king said, “well, just give us a ring!”

articles.title: Judge Asks Justice Department: Will You Oppose Trump?
articles.summary: A federal judge has ordered the Justice Department to explain whether it intends to contest President Trump’s $10 billion lawsuit against the I.R.S. over the disclosure of his tax returns.

articles.title: Supreme Court Considers Trump’s Plan to Revoke Deportation Protections
articles.summary: The Trump administration wants to terminate humanitarian protections known as Temporary Protected Status for hundreds of thousands of migrants from Haiti and Syria.

articles.title: How a Supreme Court fight over fish oil could raise your prescription drug costs
articles.summary: The justices are set to hear Hikma v. Amarin, a battle over drug patents that could raise costs for patients and change the way generic companies do business.

articles.title: It's set to be Jerome Powell's last meeting as Fed chair -- as a big change looms
articles.summary: The Fed is expected to hold rates steady, at what's likely to be Jerome Powell's last meeting as chair — with Kevin Warsh looking set to replace him.

articles.title: RFK Jr. wants to treat addiction by creating wellness farms. Does it work?
articles.summary: U.S. Secretary of Health and Human Services Robert F. Kennedy Jr. says a farm community in Italy for people with addiction is a model for wellness camps designed to ease the U.S. overdose crisis. Crit

articles.title: How a Republican state lawmaker tried to let Holocaust deniers hijack history lessons
articles.summary: A New Hampshire Republican. A German Holocaust denier. A suspicious bottle of baby oil. An NPR investigation reveals how the alarming rise of antisemitic conspiracy theories reached a state capitol.

articles.title: Could this conference be a 'turning point' for the world's use of fossil fuels?
articles.summary: Against the backdrop of an energy crisis and a warming planet, more than 50 countries have come to Santa Marta, Colombia, to discuss concrete ways to phase out oil, gas, and coal.

articles.title: Supreme Court weighs Trump's effort to end Temporary Protected Status for Haitians, Syrians
articles.summary: At issue is the TPS program, which permits eligible individuals to live and work in the United States if they cannot return to their home countries because of "extraordinary or temporary conditions."

articles.title: EU says Meta is failing to keep underage users off Facebook and Instagram
articles.summary: The European Union accused Meta on Wednesday of failing to stop underage users from accessing Facebook and Instagram, in violation of the bloc's digital rules that require sites to protect minors.

articles.title: Palestinian refugee uses art to share the story of his childhood with his daughter
articles.summary: Basel Zaraa was a Palestinian refugee. When his 5-year-old daughter started asking him questions, he told her this story.

articles.title: King Charles argues for stronger cooperation in speech to a joint meeting of Congress
articles.summary: In an address before a joint meeting of Congress on Tuesday, King Charles III sought to highlight the importance of the transatlantic relationship between the United States and Great Britain.

articles.title: How the U.K. is reacting to King Charles' visit to the U.S.
articles.summary: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Lord Peter Ricketts, a member of the House of Lords and a former British diplomat, about King Charles' visit to the U.S. during a tumultuous time.

articles.title: Will UAE's departure from OPEC affect the oil cartel's future influence?
articles.summary: What are the impacts of the UAE's decision to pull out of the OPEC oil cartel? NPR's A Martinez speaks with Helima Croft, global head of commodity strategy at RBC Capital Markets.

articles.title: Ukraine accuses Israel of importing grain 'stolen' by Russia
articles.summary: Ukraine accused Israel of allowing the import of grain it claims Russia stole from occupied areas. Israel claimed that the vessel had not entered the port and had not yet submitted its documents.

articles.title: Red cards can be given to players who cover their mouths while confronting opponents
articles.summary: The International Football Association Board approved a rule that would penalize players with a red card if they cover their mouths when confronting another player. The measure will be in place at thi

articles.title: Australia moves to tax Meta, Google and TikTok to fund newsrooms
articles.summary: Australia is proposing to tax Meta, Google and TikTok a proportion of their revenue to pay for news reporters. The government intends to introduce the draft legislation to Parliament by July.

articles.title: Gallows Humor Provides Respite From Mideast Wars
articles.summary: Arab digital creators on social media are turning out edgy material to cope with the conflicts and bring levity to a pain that often feels too raw.

articles.title: Beneath King Charles’s Jokes and Decorum, Some Subtle Rebuttals to Trump
articles.summary: King Charles III gently pushed back against President Trump’s attacks on Britain and NATO, and he spoke of the importance of checks and balances.

articles.title: DOJ Secures New James Comey Indictment Over Alleged Threat Against Trump
articles.summary: The new case stems from a social media post showing seashells on a North Carolina beach that the Trump administration characterized as a threat against the president.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
